description: Health Canada approved pembrolizumab, in combination with trastuzumab, fluoropyrimidine- and platinum-containing chemotherapy, for the first-line treatment of adult patients with locally advanced unresectable or metastatic HER2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma, whose tumours express PD-L1 (Combined Positive Score [CPS] ≥1) as determined by a validated test.

contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

indication.indication: KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab), in combination with trastuzumab, fluoropyrimidine- and platinum-containing chemotherapy,is indicated for the first-line treatment of adult patients with locally advanced unresectable or metastatic HER2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma, whose tumours express PD-L1 (Combined Positive Score [CPS] ≥1) as determined by a validated test.
